name: Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos
name_original: Subida al Alto de la Cabra — circular por la Sierra de los Vuelos
region_intro: Jogging around Quintana De La Serena offers diverse terrain across expansive plains and distinctive granite landscapes in Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by open plains interspersed with holm oaks, providing long stretches for running, alongside areas with granite outcrops and rocky trails. Historical and archaeological sites, such as pre-Roman settlements and dolmens, add a cultural dimension to many routes. The presence of riverine landscapes, particularly along the Ortigas River, also contributes to varied running environments. Best jogging routes around Quintana De La Serena The most popular jogging route is Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente la Pared by the Río Ortiga — round trip, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route follows the Río Ortigas, providing scenic views along the river. Local runners also love the Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de los Vuelos, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Jogging around Quintana De La Serena is defined by open plains, granite formations, and historical archaeological sites. The network of routes offers options for easy runs across flat terrain as well as more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Quintana De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Quintana de la Serena?
A: Quintana de la Serena offers a selection of over 10 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails range from easy paths across open plains to more challenging ascents through granite landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Quintana de la Serena?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain. The region is characterized by expansive, peaceful plains interspersed with holm oaks, offering long stretches for uninterrupted running. You'll also find distinctive granite outcrops and rocky trails, especially around areas known for granite quarries, providing varied inclines and surfaces. Some routes also follow riverine landscapes, such as along the Ortigas River.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites I can see while running?
A: Yes, Quintana de la Serena's routes often pass through or near significant historical sites. You might encounter pre-Roman fortified settlements like Hijovejo, or megalithic structures such as the Dolmen Sierra Gorda. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283332279/ascent-to-the-dolmen-of-sierra-gorda-circular-from-the-valle-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda — circular from the Valle de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; takes you directly to one of these ancient monuments. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Columns of Zalamea de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy run?
A: Yes, among the 10 available routes, there is at least 1 easy route perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed run. These typically traverse the flatter plains, offering a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty without significant elevation changes.

Q: What about more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Quintana de la Serena offers 5 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283320997/sierra-de-la-utrera-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.2 miles (22.9 km) long with considerable elevation, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283295243/ascent-to-alto-de-la-cabra-circular-through-sierra-de-los-vuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Quintana de la Serena are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283255767/puente-la-pared-by-the-rio-ortiga-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared by the Río Ortiga — round trip&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283332279/ascent-to-the-dolmen-of-sierra-gorda-circular-from-the-valle-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda — circular from the Valle de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your run.

Q: Are the running trails in Quintana de la Serena dog-friendly?
A: While specific dog-friendly policies for each trail are not always explicitly stated, the natural and open character of the plains and many trails in Quintana de la Serena generally makes them suitable for running with dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Quintana de la Serena?
A: The best time for jogging in Quintana de la Serena is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making early mornings or late evenings the only viable times for running, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow rivers or water bodies?
A: Yes, the region's proximity to the Ortigas River means some routes offer scenic views along its banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283255767/puente-la-pared-by-the-rio-ortiga-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared by the Río Ortiga — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.0-mile (12.8 km) path that follows the Río Ortigas, providing pleasant riverine landscapes for your run. The broader La Serena area is also known for large reservoirs, which contribute to the natural appeal.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Quintana de la Serena?
A: Runners often praise the tranquility and diverse landscapes of Quintana de la Serena. The combination of expansive, peaceful plains and unique granite formations provides a varied and engaging backdrop for outdoor exercise. The presence of historical sites also adds a cultural dimension to the runs, making them more than just a workout.

Q: Where can I find accommodation or places to eat after my run?
A: Quintana de la Serena, as a municipality, offers local amenities including accommodation options and places to eat. You can find various restaurants and cafes in the town center to refuel after your run. For specific details on local establishments, it's best to check local tourism resources or online travel guides for Quintana de la Serena.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer opportunities for scenic views, particularly those that involve ascents or traverse higher ground. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283295243/ascent-to-alto-de-la-cabra-circular-through-sierra-de-los-vuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos&lt;/a&gt; leads to higher elevations, potentially offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; is also a nearby highlight, suggesting areas with good viewpoints.
tips: In the Sierra de los Vuelos, the Alto de la Cabra appears, a small mountain system that gives the entrance to the first mountain ranges that are found at the southern end of Extremadura.
